title: alyssum idaeum
intro: Alyssum idaeum, also known as Creeping Veronica or Creeping Jenny, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues due to its antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Alyssum idaeum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health concerns. Herbal preparations of Alyssum idaeum typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from its leaves, stems, and roots, which can be used topically or orally to harness its medicinal properties.
